title: Medical Office Replacement Sourcing
name: Medical Office Replacement Sourcing
metaTitle: Medical Office Replacement Sourcing in San Antonio, TX
metaDescription: Lease-grade sourcing and diligence for medical office replacement property near San Antonio's Medical Center corridor, sequenced to 45-day identification deadlines.

sections.html: Medical office replacement sourcing for a San Antonio 1031 exchange starts with matching an investor's basis and debt profile against a narrow slice of clinical real estate: physician-owned buildings, health-system-leased suites, and ambulatory surgery pads clustered around the South Texas Medical Center. The shortlist is built from lease terms and tenant credit, not square footage, and it has to be ready before the 45-day identification clock runs out.

sections.heading: Medical Center Corridor Inventory
sections.html:
The South Texas Medical Center holds one of the densest concentrations of hospital and specialty-clinic real estate in San Antonio, anchoring demand for private medical office buildings within a few miles of the major systems. Physician groups that outgrow leased hospital-campus space frequently relocate into standalone MOBs nearby, which keeps a steady supply of single-tenant and small multi-tenant clinical buildings moving through the San Antonio market.

Military medical staffing adds a second demand layer. Personnel rotating through Brooke Army Medical Center at Fort Sam Houston and the medical wing at JBSA-Lackland generate consistent leasing activity for private practices near base gates, which is a factor worth weighing when comparing candidate buildings across San Antonio submarkets.

sections.heading: Lease Structure and Tenant Credit Review
sections.html:
Medical office leases vary more than retail or industrial leases in how operating costs, TI allowances, and renewal options are written, so credit review has to go beyond a headline rent number. A health-system-guaranteed lease and a solo-practitioner lease carry very different risk profiles even at the same rent per square foot, and that distinction drives most of the diligence work on a San Antonio replacement candidate.

  • Lease guarantor identity and financial strength
  • Remaining primary term versus renewal option structure
  • Tenant improvement allowance and reimbursement obligations
  • Assignment and subordination provisions tied to existing debt
  • Build-out specialization and re-tenanting cost if the space is vacated

sections.heading: 45-Day Window and Identification Sequencing
sections.html:
Because clinical buildings trade less frequently than general office stock, tours and letters of intent on San Antonio medical office candidates often need to start before the relinquished property closes. Staged identification — naming a primary candidate plus one or two backups under the three-property rule — keeps the exchange defensible if a preferred building falls out of contract during the 45-day window.

Working through a broker or advisor with active relationships across the South Texas Medical Center corridor and the smaller clinical nodes near JBSA can surface off-market or pre-marketing opportunities that never reach a public listing, which matters in a category where inventory is thin at any given time.

sections.heading: Debt and Escrow Coordination on Clinical Assets
sections.html: Lenders underwrite medical office debt around tenant credit and assignment-of-lease provisions, which means loan terms on a San Antonio replacement property can take longer to finalize than on comparable retail or multifamily deals. Coordinating those timelines with the qualified intermediary and escrow early reduces the risk of a mismatch between debt replacement and the value and equity required to avoid boot.

sections.heading: Comparable Sales and Price Benchmarking
sections.html:
Medical office pricing in San Antonio varies more by building specialization than general office pricing does, since a surgical suite with dedicated infrastructure sells on a different basis than a general-practice suite with standard build-out. Pulling recent comparable sales near the South Texas Medical Center and along secondary clinical corridors closer to JBSA gives a more accurate benchmark than applying a citywide average price per square foot.

Specialized build-out — imaging vaults, surgical infrastructure, or reinforced floor loads for equipment — can both support a premium price and narrow the pool of future buyers or tenants, which is worth weighing against the investor's expected hold period for a San Antonio medical office replacement property.

faqs.q: Does a medical office replacement property have to be leased to a hospital system to qualify?
faqs.a: No. Like-kind treatment depends on the property being held for investment, not on who occupies it. A San Antonio building leased to an independent physician group can qualify the same as one leased to a major health system, though the underwriting and credit profile differ.

faqs.q: How does military medical staffing affect demand for private medical office space in San Antonio?
faqs.a: Personnel cycling through the Brooke Army Medical Center and JBSA-Lackland medical facilities support a base of private practices near the installations. That demand layer is worth factoring into location comparisons alongside the South Texas Medical Center corridor.

faqs.q: Can a single physician-owned suite satisfy the exchange's identification requirements?
faqs.a: Yes, provided it is properly identified in writing within the 45-day window and meets the value tests the investor is relying on. Investors should confirm the specific identification language with their qualified intermediary.

faqs.q: What diligence items matter most when comparing medical office candidates?
faqs.a: Lease guarantor strength, remaining term structure, and assignment provisions tied to existing debt tend to separate strong candidates from weaker ones more than headline rent, since those terms govern how the asset performs if a tenant turns over. Specialized build-out and re-tenanting cost should also be weighed against the investor's expected hold period.

faqs.q: How early should identification work begin relative to the 45-day deadline?
faqs.a: Given how infrequently clinical buildings trade in San Antonio, tours and preliminary letters of intent often need to start before the relinquished property even closes, so the identification list is ready the day the exchange clock starts. Waiting until after closing to begin the search can leave an investor scrambling to find a qualifying building in a thin market.
